introduction:
In Andhra Pradesh, the Caste / Community Certificate is issued by the Tahsildar (Revenue Department) to certify SC, ST, BC-A, BC-B, BC-C, BC-D, BC-E (OBC), and EWS status. It is required for reservations in education, employment, welfare schemes, and elections.

The AP government provides this service both online via the Mee Seva Portal and offline at Mee Seva centres and Tahsildar offices. The applicant must provide ancestral residential proof within Andhra Pradesh.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Issued by the Tahsildar of the mandal where the family is a native
  • Processing time: 7–15 working days
  • Fee: ₹35 (via Mee Seva)
  • SC/ST certificates are permanent; BC certificates may be renewed
  • Available online via Mee Seva Portal and AP e-Pass Portal (for BC)
  • Income / Non-Creamy Layer certificate required separately for BC categories
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

Identity Proof
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID
  • Ration Card
  • Passport
Caste / Nativity Proof
  • Father's Caste Certificate
  • School Transfer Certificate (TC) mentioning caste
  • Pattadar Passbook / Land Records
  • Old Ration Card with caste entry
Additional Documents
  • Income Certificate (for BC Non-Creamy Layer)
  • Nativity / Residence Certificate
  • Passport-size photographs (2)
  • Self-declaration affidavit (if no old records)
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via Mee Seva Portal

Apply through AP Mee Seva Portal or at a Mee Seva kiosk near you.

  1. Visit Portal
    Go to meeseva.gov.in and login / register.
  2. Select Service
    Navigate to Revenue Department → Caste Certificate.
  3. Fill Application
    Enter applicant details: name, DOB, gender, father's name, caste, mandal, and district of native place.
  4. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned copies of identity proof, caste proof, and photographs.
  5. Pay Fee
    Pay ₹35 online via net banking / debit card / UPI.
  6. Submit & Track
    Submit the application, note the Transaction ID, and track status at the portal.
  7. Collect Certificate
    Download the digitally signed certificate from the portal or collect from the Mee Seva centre when ready.
BC Applicants: Also register at AP e-Pass Portal for scholarship and welfare benefits linked to BC certificate.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Mee Seva Centre / Tahsildar Office

  1. Visit Mee Seva Centre or Tahsildar Office
    Locate your nearest Mee Seva centre or go to the Tahsildar office of your native mandal.
  2. Collect & Fill Form
    Obtain the Caste Certificate application form and fill all details clearly.
  3. Submit with Documents
    Submit the form with self-attested document copies and pay ₹35 fee at the counter.
  4. Revenue Inspector Verification
    The RI (Revenue Inspector) will verify your caste and native place through a field enquiry.
  5. Collect Certificate
    Collect the signed Caste Certificate from the Tahsildar office after verification, usually within 7–15 working days.
Processing Time: 7–15 working days.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Status

Track at Mee Seva Portal using your Transaction ID.

e-Pass Registration (BC)

Register at AP e-Pass with your BC certificate to avail scholarships and reimbursements.

Grievance

Contact AP Mee Seva helpline: 1800-599-5555 or visit PG Portal.
